hawkish_phrases: current tightness of the labour market, continuing signs of robust domestic cost and price pressures, and the risk that those pressures will persist
most members of the Committee judge that some degree of further tightening in monetary policy may still be appropriate in the coming months
CPI inflation is expected to rise further over the remainder of the year, to just over 9% in 2022 Q2 and averaging slightly over 10% at its peak in 2022 Q4
underlying nominal earnings growth has risen by more than projected in the February Report and is expected to strengthen in coming months
risks of more persistent strength in nominal wage growth and domestic price setting
dovish_phrases: material deterioration in the outlook for world and UK growth
squeeze on real disposable incomes is starting to weigh on the household sector
unemployment rate is expected to rise to 5½% in three years' time given the sharp slowdown in demand growth
excess supply builds to 2¼% by the end of the forecast period
CPI inflation is projected to fall...to 1.3% in three years, well below the target
summary: The MPC voted 6-3 to raise Bank Rate by 25bp to 1.0%, with three members preferring a 50bp hike, citing tight labour markets and robust domestic cost and price pressures despite a material deterioration in the growth outlook, and most members judging that further tightening may still be appropriate in coming months.
